user.title: Solar Energy Management Setup

data.observer_mode: Test mode (read-only — do not control hardware)

data_description.observer_mode: Leave OFF for a normal install. Turn ON only on a test/staging Home Assistant that mirrors a production instance, so SEM never sends commands to KEBA, the inverter, or any switches.

ev_charger.title: EV Charger
ev_charger.description: SEM auto-detected your EV charger from the Home Assistant entity registry. Review the entries below and click **Submit** — there is usually nothing to change. Optional fields can be left blank.

data.ev_connected_sensor: 🔌 EV Connected Status — vehicle plug connection
data.ev_charging_sensor: ⚡ EV Charging Active — current charging state
data.ev_charging_power_sensor: 🚗 EV Charging Power — power delivered to vehicle (W)
data.ev_total_energy_sensor: 🔋 EV Total Energy — cumulative energy delivered (kWh)

data_description.ev_connected_sensor: Binary sensor that reports whether the vehicle plug is currently connected. Auto-detected from KEBA / Easee / Wallbox / go-eCharger / Zaptec / ChargePoint / Heidelberg.
data_description.ev_charging_sensor: Binary sensor that reports whether SEM should consider charging active. Auto-detected from your charger integration.
data_description.ev_charging_power_sensor: Numeric sensor (W) reporting how much power is currently flowing into the vehicle. Auto-detected from your charger integration.
data_description.ev_charger_service: Home Assistant service called to set the charging current (e.g. keba.set_current, easee.set_charger_max_limit). Auto-filled for KEBA. Leave blank for chargers controlled via a number entity.
data_description.ev_charger_service_entity_id: Entity ID passed as the target of the service call. Auto-filled when needed; leave blank if the service is global.
data_description.ev_current_sensor: Optional. Numeric sensor (A) reporting the actual charging current. Used for diagnostics.
data_description.ev_total_energy_sensor: Optional. Cumulative kWh counter for energy delivered to the vehicle. Used for accurate session tracking.

hardware.title: Hardware
hardware.description:
Two final values that depend on your installation:

• Home Battery Capacity — the usable kWh of your inverter battery.
• Peak Power Limit — the maximum kW your grid contract allows before SEM starts shedding load.

SEM also auto-detected your inverter's discharge control entity: {discharge_entity}

Leave the dashboard option ON to have SEM build its Lovelace dashboard for you. **Note: this triggers a Home Assistant restart about 5 seconds after install completes.**

Everything else uses safe defaults that you can tune later from the integration's options.

data.generate_dashboard_on_install: Generate the SEM dashboard now (restarts HA)

data_description.battery_capacity_kwh: Total usable capacity of your home battery (NOT the EV) in kWh. Used by the forecast-driven charging strategy to estimate how much battery can bridge a solar deficit. Check your inverter spec.
data_description.target_peak_limit: Maximum kW your grid contract allows before SEM begins load-shedding. For demand-based tariffs typically 3–5 kW; otherwise set to your fuse rating divided by 4 to leave headroom.
data_description.generate_dashboard_on_install: When enabled, SEM creates the Lovelace dashboard immediately after this step finishes and then restarts Home Assistant ~5 s later so the new dashboard becomes visible. You can always re-run it manually later via the solar_energy_management.generate_dashboard service.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure Solar Energy Management
reconfigure.description: Update your EV charger and notification settings

error.unknown: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.
error.entity_not_found: Entity {entity_id} was not found. Please check that the entity exists and is spelled correctly.
error.incorrect_device_class: Entity {entity_id} has incorrect device_class {actual}, expected {expected}. Please select a sensor with the correct device class.
error.incorrect_state_class: Entity {entity_id} has incorrect state_class {actual}, expected {expected}. Energy sensors must use state_class: total_increasing.
error.entity_unavailable: Entity {entity_id} is currently unavailable. Please ensure the device is online and providing data.
error.invalid_value: Entity {entity_id} has an invalid value {value}. Expected a numeric value.
error.service_not_found: Notification service {service} was not found. Please check the service name (e.g., notify.mobile_app_your_phone).
error.validation_failed: Validation failed for one or more sensors. Please check the error messages below and correct the issues.
error.solar_production_sensor: Solar production sensor is required and must provide power readings in Watts (W).
error.grid_power_sensor: Grid power sensor is required and must provide power readings in Watts (W). Negative = import, Positive = export.
error.battery_soc_sensor: Battery SOC sensor is required and must provide percentage readings (0-100%).
error.battery_power_sensor: Battery power sensor is required and must provide power readings in Watts (W). Positive = charging, Negative = discharging.
error.ev_connected_sensor: EV connected sensor is required and must be a binary_sensor (ON/OFF).
error.ev_charging_sensor: EV charging sensor is required and must be a binary_sensor (ON/OFF).
error.ev_charging_power_sensor: EV charging power sensor is required and must provide power readings in Watts (W).

abort.already_configured: Solar Energy Management is already configured. Only one instance is allowed.

ev_charger.title: Cargador VE
ev_charger.description: Revise y actualice las asignaciones de entidades de su cargador de VE. Los campos de SOC del vehículo solo se utilizan si proporciona un sensor de SOC de batería del vehículo.

data.ev_charger_service: Servicio de ajuste de corriente del cargador VE (opcional)
data.ev_charger_service_entity_id: Entidad destino del servicio del cargador VE (opcional)

data_description.vehicle_soc_entity: Optional. A sensor that exposes the vehicle's current State of Charge (0–100 %). Only a few cars provide this (Tesla, some VW IDs, MG ZS EV with custom integrations, OBD-II adapters). If empty, SEM uses the kWh-based daily target instead and the two fields below are ignored.
data_description.ev_battery_capacity_kwh: Total usable capacity of the EV battery. ONLY used when the Vehicle SOC sensor above is configured — SEM combines it with the SOC reading to compute the remaining energy needed to reach the target. Without a SOC sensor this value is ignored entirely.
data_description.ev_target_soc: SOC % SEM aims to reach when night charging. ONLY used when the Vehicle SOC sensor is configured. Without one, the kWh-based 'Daily EV target' field on the Optimization Settings step is used instead.

data_description.update_interval: How often the coordinator reads sensors and adjusts hardware. 10 s gives the most accurate energy integration; values above 30 s will under-count energy. Range 10–60 s.
data_description.power_delta: Change-detection threshold for power sensors. Lower = more updates (more sensitive but heavier on the recorder). Default 1000 W is fine for most installs; tune down to 100–200 W for finer control.
data_description.current_delta: Minimum charging-current change (A) before SEM updates the EV charger. Higher = less ramping.
data_description.soc_delta: Minimum battery SOC change (%) before SEM reacts. Battery SOC moves slowly so 10 % is usually fine.
data_description.battery_priority_soc: Zone 1 → Zone 2 boundary. Below this SOC all solar goes to the battery and the EV is blocked. Default 30 %. The four-zone strategy (Architecture docs) needs this LOW (5–60 %) — do NOT confuse with the legacy 3-zone meaning where this was a high target.
data_description.battery_buffer_soc: Zone 2 → Zone 3 boundary. Above this SOC the battery may discharge to bridge solar deficits while charging the EV. Default 70 %.
data_description.battery_auto_start_soc: Zone 3 → Zone 4 boundary. Above this SOC the EV can start charging even with no solar surplus, drawing from the battery. Default 90 %.
data_description.battery_assist_floor_soc: Hysteresis floor — once battery assist is active, it stays active until SOC drops below this value. Default 60 %.
data_description.min_solar_power: Surplus floor below which solar EV charging will not even attempt to start. Keep well below the EV charger's hardware minimum (~4140 W on 3-phase, ~1380 W on 1-phase).
data_description.max_grid_import: Hard cap on grid power that SEM will pull while doing solar charging. 0 = pure-PV mode (the recommended SEM default).
data_description.daily_ev_target: Target kWh per day for night-grid charging. Set to 0 to disable night charging entirely.
data_description.battery_assist_max_power: Maximum power the home battery can discharge to assist the EV in Zone 3/4. Default 4500 W.
data_description.battery_capacity_kwh: Total usable home battery capacity (NOT the EV battery — that's a separate field on the EV step). Used by the forecast-driven charging strategy.
data_description.battery_discharge_protection_enabled: When ON, SEM caps the inverter's discharge limit during night EV charging so the home battery only powers the house (not the EV).
data_description.battery_max_discharge_power: Hard upper limit on battery discharge enforced by the protection feature.
data_description.electricity_import_rate: Day / flat tariff (CHF/kWh, incl. VAT). Used for cost tracking.
data_description.electricity_off_peak_rate: Night tariff (CHF/kWh). Set equal to import rate if you don't have a dual tariff.
data_description.electricity_export_rate: Feed-in rate paid by the utility (CHF/kWh).
data_description.demand_charge_rate: Monthly demand charge per kW of peak (CHF/kW/month). Set to 0 if your contract has no demand charge.
data_description.observer_mode: When ON, SEM monitors and calculates everything but never sends a service call to hardware. Use on a test/staging HA mirroring a production install.
data_description.smart_night_charging: When ON, SEM intelligently skips or reduces night charging based on estimated EV SOC, solar forecast, temperature-adjusted consumption prediction, and learned driving patterns.
data_description.battery_discharge_control_entity: Number entity that controls the inverter's discharge power limit (e.g. number.batteries_maximale_entladeleistung on Huawei). Auto-detected on install; override here if needed.

settings_ev.title: Carga VE y Solar
settings_ev.description: Configurar objetivos de carga del VE y umbrales solares.

data.min_solar_power: Potencia solar mínima para iniciar carga VE (W)
data.max_grid_import: Importación máxima de red durante carga solar (W, 0 = solo solar)
data.observer_mode: Modo observador (solo lectura, sin control de hardware)
data.smart_night_charging: Reducir objetivo nocturno según previsión de mañana

settings_tariff.title: Tarifas y Avanzado
settings_tariff.description: Tarifas eléctricas y parámetros avanzados de ajuste.

dashboard_options.title: Dashboard Installation
dashboard_options.description: Install or reinstall the SEM dashboard

data_description.generate_dashboard: Create a new Lovelace dashboard for SEM. If a dashboard with the same path already exists, it will be replaced.
data_description.dashboard_title: Display name for the dashboard in the sidebar. Example: 'Solar Energy Management'
data_description.dashboard_path: URL path for the dashboard (lowercase, use dashes). Example: 'sem-dashboard'
data_description.include_financial: Add a financial tracking tab showing costs, savings, and ROI metrics.
data_description.include_flows: Add a power flow visualization tab with Sankey diagram showing energy flows.
